ops.title: Judge prompt v5 (triage.py) fixes four v4 directive-vs-pushback boundary misses with surgical rules: (1) removal/reversal orders undoing the assistant's OWN just-added work are pushback (unwanted_action), but removing pre-existing state is a plain directive; (2) a constraint-violation clause inside a forward directive ('we arent supposed to be using litellm anymore') is corrective via the mixed rule; (3) a leading 'no/nope' that merely answers the assistant's question or declines an offered option is noise, not rejection; (4) in review_comment, bare prohibitions/conventions ('no comments', 'it is required') are style_violation corrections. Validated 49/49 on golden_triage.json at repeat-3 opus majority through the production judge path (run_verdicts/structured_judge). Bumping PROMPT_VERSION 4->5 forces a full corpus re-triage.
